name: pr-to-dev
description: Partition in-scope changes into atomic commits, publish one PR targeting dev, wait for fresh required checks, merge through protected policy, reconcile the remote, and clean only run-owned resources.

acceptance.description: Every in-scope change is included in an intentional atomic commit batch, or an explicit exclusion is recorded; no silent leftovers remain.
Each commit has a reviewed scope, passes applicable validation, and preserves a usable rollback sequence.
The current PR targets the exact protected dev branch and its head revision matches the reviewed commit history.
All required checks for the current PR head pass or are otherwise reconciled according to repository policy; stale checks do not authorize merge.
The PR is merged through the repository's protected policy without admin bypass, and the provider reports a terminal merge result.
The local and remote dev revisions are reconciled after merge, with the provider receipt and final revision recorded.
Only branches, worktrees, temporary artifacts, or PR resources owned by this run are cleaned after merge and remote reconciliation.
